# Estimating Right-Foot Steps onto the Step in a TBC Class

## My Point Estimate: **750**

## Reasoning

**Class structure assumptions (Total Body Conditioning with step platform):**
- Total class duration: ~55 minutes
- Active stepping time: ~33–35 minutes (subtracting warm-up, cool-down, stretching, strength segments, water breaks, transitions)

**Tempo and choreography:**
- Typical step-class music tempo: ~128–132 BPM → ~130 BPM average
- Most choreography works in 4-count phrases → ~32.5 phrases per minute

**Right-foot frequency per phrase:**
- When the **right foot leads** (~50% of choreography): 1 right-foot step onto the platform per phrase
- When the **left foot leads** (~50%): right foot still steps on in two-footed moves (basic, V-step) but **not** in single-leg moves (knee lifts, kicks)
- Weighted average: **~0.70–0.75** right-foot steps onto the step per 4-count phrase

**Calculation:**
| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Active stepping minutes | ~34 min |
| Phrases per minute | ~32.5 |
| R-foot steps per phrase | ~0.68 (adjusted for transitions/rests) |
| **Total** | **34 × 32.5 × 0.68 ≈ 750** |

# Estimating Right-Foot Steps onto the Step in a TBC Class

## My Estimate: **750**

## Reasoning

**Class Structure (Total Body Conditioning, ~55 min):**
- Warm-up/cool-down: ~10 min (some light stepping)
- Step-based cardio blocks: ~25–30 min
- Strength/core/floor segments: ~15–20 min (little to no step contact)
- **Effective stepping time: ~30 minutes**

**Step Tempo & Choreography:**
- Typical step music tempo: ~128–132 BPM
- A basic step cycle (R-up, L-up, R-down, L-down) = **4 beats → ~32 cycles/min**
- The right foot steps **onto** the step once per cycle regardless of which foot leads
- However, many moves use 8-beat patterns (V-steps, over-the-tops, repeaters), and some involve taps/floor work → average effective rate drops

**Adjustment factors:**
- Mixed choreography complexity: ~0.85
- Transitions, water breaks, learning new combos: ~0.85
- Combined efficiency: **~0.72**

**Calculation:**
$$32.5 \text{ steps/min} \times 30 \text{ min} \times 0.72 \approx \boxed{700–750}$$

Rounding to my single best estimate: **750 right-foot steps onto the step**.

- Active stepping segments: ~25–30 minutes
- Strength/floor work/rest/transitions: ~20–25 minutes

**Stepping rate during active segments:**
- Typical step music tempo: ~128–132 BPM
- A standard 4-count pattern (basic step, V-step, etc.) yields **one right-foot step-up per pattern**
- At ~130 BPM → ~32 patterns/min → ~32 right-foot step-ups/min in *continuous* stepping
- Adjusting for repeaters, transitions, directional changes, water breaks, and mixed choreography → effective rate of **~20 right-foot step-ups per active minute**

**Calculation:**
| Segment | Duration | Rate (R-foot/min) | Subtotal |
|---|---|---|---|
| Warm-up (light stepping) | 5 min | ~10 | 50 |
| Cardio/step tracks | 22 min | ~20 | 440 |
| Mixed athletic/step | 5 min | ~12 | 60 |
| Strength/cooldown | ~23 min | ~0 | 0 |
| **Total** | | | **~550** |

## My Estimate

**550**
